title: Auto-Improvement Framework Schemas
description: Canonical schemas for the auto-improvement framework. Companion to docs/workflow/auto_improvement_framework.md. Covers workflow.json extensions, planning/metrics.json, structured builder/improve.md / builder/improve-archive decision entries, and the LLM-callable metric tool inputs.

OversightMode.description: Per-workflow oversight mode. manual = every change requires approval; supervised = low-risk auto, high-risk gated; autonomous = LLM applies allowed changes without gates.

DecisionLogMutability.description: append_only allows superseding edits via append; append_only_strict forbids any edit, even corrective. Used by compliance workflows.

MetricMode.description: target = drive toward a number; slo = stay above floor / below ceiling.

MetricRole.description: primary = the small set of north-star or must-not-break metrics that steer optimization; secondary = diagnostic/supporting metrics that explain or protect primary movement.

type.description: Two source types: eval_step (value computed by a Python eval step) or telemetry (engine-recorded run data like cost or duration). For external feeds, schema validation, lineage, or delayed-outcome attribution, write a Python eval step that does the work and use type=eval_step.

id.description: For type=eval_step, the eval step's id (must exist in evaluation/evaluation_plan.json).

field.description: For type=telemetry, the dotted path to the value (e.g., run.total_cost_usd). For type=eval_step, optional field key into the step's structured JSON output (omit to read the step's percent score).

id.description: Unique within planning/metrics.json. kebab.dot pattern (e.g., audit.accuracy).

category.description: Freeform grouping for UI and optimizer reasoning, e.g. outcome, execution, guardrail, content_quality, strategy_learning, telemetry.

unit.description: percent | usd | seconds | count | days | ratio | bps | etc.

success_criteria.description: The soul.md success criterion this metric operationalizes. Quote or summarize the exact criterion; UI warns when this is absent.

parent.description: Optional id of a parent metric for grouping in UI. NO weighted rollup.

version.description: Incremented on amend. Series prior to this version are archived; chart renderers should break the line at the version transition.

archive.description: Prior versions of amended metrics. Append-only.

active_mode.description: Runtime state for dual-mode workflows (declared in builder/improve.md's Workflow Profile). The active mode value (e.g. 'explore' / 'exploit' for social-media; mode names are workflow-defined) — steps branch on this via the variable resolver. Empty for single-mode workflows.

WorkflowExtensions.description: Additions to workflow.json that the auto-improvement framework and runtime read as structured config. Workflow profile (primary type, secondary traits, plan stability, runtime mode, business-context accumulation) lives as prose in builder/improve.md under a '## Workflow Profile' section — the agent reads improve.md every turn anyway, and prose captures nuance enums can't.

run_retention_count.description: Number of backup run iterations to keep in runs/ and evaluation/runs/, excluding iteration-0. Defaults to 5 when omitted.

DecisionSource.description: Who made this decision. agent = builder/optimizer LLM; user = human override or capture-context; system = automated framework action.

DecisionEntry.description: One structured improve-decision fenced block in the improve ledger: builder/improve.md for recent/current entries or builder/improve-archive/YYYY-MM.md for archived old entries. Append-only at the ledger level.

trigger.description: Origin of the decision. Slash command name (e.g., improve-evaluation, capture-context), system action name, or override action.

applied_changes.description: Workflow-relative file paths that were modified.

target_metrics.description: Metric ids this decision is meant to move. Required for business-context capture and metric-linked improvement decisions.

linked_review_finding.description: builder/review.md finding ids (F-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN) this decision resolves or partially resolves.

linked_improve_entry.description: builder/improve.md proposal ids (I-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN) this decision resolves or implements.

regulation_ref.description: For compliance workflows: reference to the regulation/clause this decision relates to.

evidence_paths.description: For compliance: pointers to forensic evidence supporting the decision.

supersedes.description: Decision id this entry supersedes (used for corrections in append_only mode).

rule_added.description: Context capture: the user-supplied runtime context appended to knowledgebase/context/context.md. Populated when source=user + trigger=capture-context.

rule_section.description: Context capture: the markdown section heading the context was added under.

example_paths.description: Context capture: workflow-relative paths under knowledgebase/context/examples/ associated with this captured context.

ToolInput_propose_metric.description: Input schema for the propose_metric tool. Used by the proposer.

category.description: Freeform grouping for UI and optimizer reasoning, e.g. outcome, execution, guardrail, content_quality, strategy_learning, telemetry.

success_criteria.description: The soul.md success criterion this metric operationalizes. Quote or summarize the exact criterion.

amend_existing.description: Set when amending an existing active metric id. The previous active definition is archived and the new active definition gets version+1.

ToolInput_capture_context.description: Input schema for the capture_context tool. Used after user confirmation to persist durable runtime business context.

section.description: Markdown section heading in knowledgebase/context/context.md. Defaults to General when empty.

context_text.description: The durable user-supplied context to capture.

target_metrics.description: Active metric ids this context is expected to affect.

example_note.description: Optional short note about the example or source context for this capture.
